title: CONVENTIONS.toml parsing, ConventionsConfig type, and DiagnosticInfra integration
purpose: CONVENTIONS.toml is the foundation of the entire conventions system. Without a parsed config type and its integration into DiagnosticInfra, no convention group can be matched, no advisory/blocking distinction can be applied, and no convention-driven checks can run. This brief delivers the config parsing layer and wires it into the existing diagnostic infrastructure so subsequent briefs can read the config and act on it.
task: Create a conventions module under crates/norn/src/tools/ containing the ConventionsConfig, ConventionGroup, LocCheck, Handling, and ConventionsError types. Implement TOML parsing with glob pattern pre-compilation. Add the conventions: Option<ConventionsConfig> field to DiagnosticInfra. Wire the CONVENTIONS.toml load into session startup in all three driver paths (TUI, REPL, print). When no CONVENTIONS.toml exists, conventions is None and no convention-driven checks run. When the file exists but is malformed, session startup logs an error and continues with None.

requirements.title: Conventions module structure
requirements.spec: A conventions/ folder module SHALL exist at crates/norn/src/tools/conventions/ with mod.rs containing only pub mod and pub use statements. The module SHALL contain config.rs (types and parsing) and error.rs (ConventionsError type).
acceptance: crates/norn/src/tools/conventions/ directory exists
crates/norn/src/tools/conventions/mod.rs contains only pub mod and pub use statements
crates/norn/src/tools/conventions/config.rs contains ConventionsConfig, ConventionGroup, LocCheck, Handling types
crates/norn/src/tools/conventions/error.rs contains ConventionsError type
crates/norn/src/tools/mod.rs declares pub mod conventions and re-exports ConventionsConfig

requirements.title: ConventionGroup struct
requirements.spec: ConventionGroup SHALL have fields: tools (Vec<String>), paths (Vec<String>), loc (Option<LocCheck>), advise_on (Vec<String> defaulting to empty), block_on (Vec<String> defaulting to empty), bypass_detection (Option<bool>), adapters_dir (Option<PathBuf>), priority (i32 defaulting to 0). LocCheck SHALL have fields: limit (u64), handling (Handling). Handling SHALL be an enum with Advise and Block variants. All types SHALL derive Deserialize, Debug, and Clone.
acceptance: ConventionGroup has all 8 fields with correct types
LocCheck has limit: u64 and handling: Handling fields
Handling enum has Advise and Block variants
advise_on, block_on default to empty Vec via serde(default)
priority defaults to 0 via serde(default)
All types derive Deserialize, Debug, Clone
Test: deserialize a TOML string with all fields populated, verify struct values match

requirements.title: ConventionsConfig parsing
requirements.spec: ConventionsConfig SHALL be a struct wrapping a BTreeMap<String, ConventionGroup> (group name to group). ConventionsConfig::load(path: &Path) SHALL read the file and parse it with the toml crate. On success it SHALL pre-compile all glob patterns from each group's paths field using the globset crate, storing CompiledConventionGroup values alongside the parsed data. On failure it SHALL return ConventionsError with variants: FileNotFound, ParseError(String), InvalidGlob { group: String, pattern: String, reason: String }.
acceptance: ConventionsConfig::load reads a TOML file from disk and parses it into ConventionsConfig
ConventionsConfig stores compiled glob matchers alongside parsed groups
ConventionsError::FileNotFound returned when CONVENTIONS.toml does not exist
ConventionsError::ParseError returned when TOML is malformed
ConventionsError::InvalidGlob returned when a paths glob cannot compile
ConventionsError derives Debug and implements std::error::Error via thiserror
Test: load a valid CONVENTIONS.toml from a tempfile, verify groups and compiled matchers
Test: load a nonexistent path, verify FileNotFound
Test: load malformed TOML, verify ParseError

requirements.title: LOC counting via tokei
requirements.spec: ConventionsConfig SHALL use the file_length adapter's count_code_lines function from the diagnostics crate for LOC counting. This is the same tokei-based counting already used by the current hardcoded check. The ConventionGroup's loc.limit SHALL be compared against this count.
acceptance: LOC counting delegates to diagnostics::languages::file_length::count_code_lines
No raw line counting (wc -l style) anywhere in conventions code
Test: create a Rust file with known LOC, verify count_code_lines returns the expected value

requirements.title: DiagnosticInfra gains conventions field
requirements.spec: DiagnosticInfra in crates/norn/src/tools/diagnostics_check.rs SHALL gain a pub conventions: Option<ConventionsConfig> field. The field SHALL be None when no CONVENTIONS.toml exists.
acceptance: DiagnosticInfra has pub conventions: Option<ConventionsConfig> field
Existing code that constructs DiagnosticInfra compiles with the new field set to None
All existing tests pass unchanged

requirements.title: Session startup wiring
requirements.spec: WHEN session startup constructs DiagnosticInfra, it SHALL attempt to load CONVENTIONS.toml from the workspace root. IF the file exists and parses successfully, conventions SHALL be set to Some(config). IF the file does not exist, conventions SHALL be None. IF the file exists but fails to parse, a warning SHALL be logged via tracing::warn and conventions SHALL be None. All three driver paths (TUI driver.rs, REPL driver.rs, print orchestrator.rs) or the shared wiring function they call SHALL include this logic.
acceptance: CONVENTIONS.toml is loaded during session startup from workspace_root
Missing file results in conventions: None with no warning
Malformed file results in conventions: None with tracing::warn
Valid file results in conventions: Some(parsed_config)
All three drivers (TUI, REPL, print) construct DiagnosticInfra with the conventions field
grep for 'ConventionsConfig::load' in the startup path returns matches

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement convention matching logic — that is NTC-002
SHALL NOT implement advisory/blocking pathway or PostCheckResult — that is NTC-003
SHALL NOT modify DiagnosticsPostCheck::check() behavior — that is NTC-002
SHALL NOT implement the init command — that is NTC-004
SHALL NOT remove the hardcoded checks from diagnostics_check.rs — that is NTC-002
SHALL NOT add todo!() or unimplemented!() macros anywhere
verification: crates/norn/src/tools/conventions/ directory exists with mod.rs, config.rs, error.rs
crates/norn/src/tools/conventions/mod.rs contains only pub mod and pub use statements
grep -r 'ConventionsConfig::load' returns matches in session startup wiring
No file exceeds 500 lines of code (excluding tests, comments, whitespace)
